text:
SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT

DEPARTMENT OF PATHOLOGY AND LABORATORY MEDICINE

---

PATIENT: Margaret Choi
ACCESSION NUMBER: SP-XXXX-XXXXX
SUBMITTING PHYSICIAN: Gastroenterology / Endoscopy Unit
PROCEDURE DATE: [DATE]
REPORT DATE: [DATE]
CLINICAL HISTORY: 58-year-old female with 6-week history of new-onset mid-epigastric pain radiating to the back, 12-lb unintentional weight loss over 2 months, progressive jaundice, and newly poorly controlled type 2 diabetes mellitus. Multiphasic CT abdomen/pelvis (pancreatic protocol) demonstrated a 3.8 cm hypoenhancing mass in the head/uncinate process of the pancreas with borderline resectable features including approximately 180-degree SMA abutment and SMV/portal vein confluence abutment. Biliary obstruction with common bile duct dilation to 14 mm and intrahepatic biliary ductal dilation present. EUS-guided fine needle biopsy performed for tissue diagnosis.

---

SPECIMEN SUBMITTED:

Specimen A: EUS-FNB, pancreatic head mass, core needle biopsy

---

GROSS DESCRIPTION:

Specimen A: Received in formalin are three tan-white cylindrical core biopsy fragments ranging in aggregate length from 0.4 cm to 1.2 cm. The cores are firm and demonstrate a gritty, whitish cut surface consistent with desmoplastic tissue. The fragments are entirely submitted in one cassette (A1), embedded in one block. Two additional passes were submitted as cell block material (A2). Representative smears were evaluated at the time of the procedure for adequacy by the attending pathologist (on-site rapid assessment confirmed cellular specimen adequate for diagnosis).

Total tissue submitted: Entirely submitted.

---

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION AND DIAGNOSIS:

SPECIMEN A: EUS-FNB, PANCREATIC HEAD MASS, CORE NEEDLE BIOPSY

DIAGNOSIS: PANCREATIC DUCTAL ADENOCARCINOMA, MODERATELY DIFFERENTIATED.

Microscopic examination of the core biopsy fragments reveals infiltrating neoplastic glands embedded within an abundant desmoplastic stromal reaction. The tumor glands are irregularly shaped with haphazard architecture and abrupt luminal contour irregularities. The neoplastic cells are columnar to cuboidal with moderate to abundant pale eosinophilic cytoplasm. Nuclei are enlarged (2-4x the size of a normal ductal cell nucleus), vesicular, and demonstrate prominent nucleoli in a subset. There is marked nuclear pleomorphism with irregular nuclear membranes and loss of nuclear polarity. Mitotic figures are readily identified, with occasional atypical mitoses noted. Intraluminal and intracytoplasmic mucin production is prominent, with focal extracellular mucin pools. The stroma is composed of dense collagenous fibrosis with scattered activated fibroblasts and myofibroblasts, chronic inflammatory infiltrate, and occasional macrophages. Perineural invasion is focally identified within the biopsy fragments. No vascular invasion is definitively identified in the limited sampling. No normal residual pancreatic acinar tissue is identified adjacent to the tumor in the cores. A separate cell block preparation demonstrates clusters of malignant glandular cells with similar morphology, consistent with the core biopsy findings.

Differential diagnosis was considered and includes: metastatic adenocarcinoma to the pancreas, autoimmune pancreatitis (excluded by lack of lymphoplasmacytic infiltrate and IgG4-positive plasma cells, and presence of clearly malignant cytologic features), and well-differentiated neuroendocrine neoplasm (excluded on morphologic and IHC grounds, see below). The histologic and immunohistochemical features are most consistent with a pancreatic primary ductal adenocarcinoma.

---

ANCILLARY STUDIES:

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY (Performed on block A1):

The following immunohistochemical stains were performed using validated antibody clones on formalin-fixed, paraffin-embedded tissue with appropriate positive and negative controls:

  - CK7 (cytokeratin 7): POSITIVE -- Diffuse, strong cytoplasmic staining of neoplastic cells.
  - CK20 (cytokeratin 20): NEGATIVE -- No staining of neoplastic cells. Positive internal control (colonic mucosa) confirmed.
  - MUC1 (mucin 1, EMA): POSITIVE -- Diffuse, strong membranous and cytoplasmic expression in neoplastic cells.
  - MUC2 (mucin 2): NEGATIVE -- No goblet cell-type mucin expression in neoplastic cells.
  - CDX2: NEGATIVE -- No intestinal differentiation marker expression in neoplastic cells.
  - SMAD4 / DPC4 (clone B-8): NEGATIVE (LOSS OF EXPRESSION) -- Complete absence of nuclear SMAD4 staining in neoplastic cells. Internal positive control (stromal cells, vascular endothelium, and entrapped non-neoplastic ductal epithelium) shows intact nuclear staining, confirming adequate immunoreactivity of the stain. This pattern is consistent with biallelic inactivation of the SMAD4 tumor suppressor gene and is characteristic of a subset of pancreatic ductal adenocarcinomas.
  - Synaptophysin: NEGATIVE -- Excludes neuroendocrine differentiation.
  - Chromogranin A: NEGATIVE -- Excludes neuroendocrine differentiation.
  - p53 (clone DO-7): ABERRANT STAINING PATTERN -- Diffuse, strong nuclear overexpression noted in the majority of neoplastic cells (>70%), raising strong suspicion for a TP53 gain-of-function missense mutation. This finding is consistent with loss of normal tumor suppressor function and is a common finding in pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma.

KI-67 PROLIFERATION INDEX (MIB-1):
  Approximately 35% of neoplastic cell nuclei demonstrate immunoreactivity, indicating a high proliferative index consistent with moderately to poorly differentiated carcinoma.

MISMATCH REPAIR (MMR) PROTEIN IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
  - MLH1: RETAINED (intact nuclear expression)
  - PMS2: RETAINED (intact nuclear expression)
  - MSH2: RETAINED (intact nuclear expression)
  - MSH6: RETAINED (intact nuclear expression)
  Interpretation: No loss of mismatch repair protein expression detected. This pattern is consistent with mismatch repair-proficient (pMMR) tumor. These findings argue against microsatellite instability-high (MSI-H) status; confirmatory PCR-based MSI testing may be performed at the discretion of the treating team (PENDING, see note below).

NOTE - PENDING STUDIES:
MSI by PCR fragment analysis has been ordered on this specimen and results are PENDING at the time of this report. An addendum will be issued upon completion. In addition, a reflex referral for comprehensive tumor genomic profiling (next-generation sequencing) has been placed per institutional protocol for pancreatic adenocarcinoma; tissue has been reserved for this purpose.

---

SUMMARY / INTEGRATED INTERPRETATION:

The morphologic, histochemical, and immunohistochemical findings are diagnostic of moderately differentiated ductal adenocarcinoma arising from the pancreatic head. The immunoprofile (CK7+/CK20-/CDX2-/MUC1+/MUC2-) confirms pancreatic/upper gastrointestinal biliary-type glandular differentiation and effectively excludes a colorectal or lower gastrointestinal primary. Loss of SMAD4/DPC4 protein expression by IHC is a well-established marker of biallelic SMAD4 inactivation, which occurs in approximately 55% of pancreatic ductal adenocarcinomas and is associated with a propensity for early hematogenous (particularly hepatic) metastasis. The aberrant p53 pattern is consistent with TP53 missense mutation. These molecular alterations in combination with the classic desmoplastic morphology are characteristic of pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma.

Grading: Moderately differentiated (Grade 2 of 3, WHO classification). Based on gland formation: >50% of tumor demonstrates recognizable glandular architecture with areas of less-organized infiltrative growth. Nuclear features and mitotic activity support moderate-to-high grade biology.

Correlation with clinical presentation and imaging findings (3.8 cm pancreatic head/uncinate mass with borderline resectable vascular involvement on CT) is recommended.

---

ELECTRONIC SIGN-OUT:

Attending Pathologist: [Pathologist Name, MD]
Department of Anatomic Pathology

Resident/Fellow: [Resident Name, MD]

Dictated: [DATE]
Transcribed: [DATE]
Signed: [DATE]

---

ADDENDUM (if applicable): MSI PCR results and NGS referral status will be provided as a separate addendum upon availability.

---
End of Report
